# Cold Handoff — `openclown-assistant` Orchestrator → New Devin Account

**Generated:** 2026-04-30 21:53 UTC, auto-cold after the close of the **ADR-010** (Calorie Floor on Final Target) review cycle.
**Type:** COLD (auto-generated, formal state only — no texture / no observations).
**Handoff target:** Fresh Devin session in any account that can be authenticated to the `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` repo.
**Outgoing session:** `https://app.devin.ai/sessions/2ff3c66567464a138ddb3be9e89a26a1` (continuation of warm-handoff `2026-04-30-session-2-warm` produced by previous orchestrator).

---

## 0. What you (new Devin) are looking at

You are taking over the **AI-engineering mentor / orchestrator** role for `openclown-assistant`. This file is your full bootstrap. Read every section before touching the repo.

Your three core obligations (full prompt in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md`, read it after the self-checks in §1):

1. **Teach** the PO (non-engineer founder learning the SDLC).
2. **Push back** with evidence; cite artifacts and ADRs. Sycophancy is a failure mode.
3. **Protect docs-as-code:** every architectural decision must live in a versioned markdown file in git. "We agreed in chat" is not a decision.

You are NOT one of the four pipeline LLM agents (Business Planner, Architect, Executor, Reviewer). You are the conductor.

---

## 1. Self-check (run BEFORE asking the PO anything)

### 1a. `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN`

Call:

```
secrets action="list"
```

If you see `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN` (or fallback names like `GITHUB_PAT_TOKEN2`, `GITHUB_PAT`, `GH_TOKEN`) → OK, skip to 1b.

If none present, request via:

```
secrets action="request" secret_name="GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN" type="plain" should_save=true save_scope="user" note="GitHub fine-grained PAT for OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant: Contents R/W + Pull requests R/W + Workflows R/W"
```

Send a non-blocking message to the PO explaining what scopes the PAT needs.

**Note (delta from session-1):** in the outgoing session this secret was NOT present, but the Devin native git proxy (`git-manager.devin.ai/proxy/github.com/...`) authenticated `git fetch` / `git push` directly without it. The PAT is only needed if you must use `gh` CLI as a fallback OR if your account lacks the native git integration. The repo is public so read-only `git clone` works without any token.

### 1b. Repo clone

```bash
[[ -d ~/repos/openclown-assistant/.git ]] && echo "REPO_OK" || echo "REPO_MISSING"
```

If `REPO_OK` → `cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant && git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only origin main`.

If `REPO_MISSING`:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/repos
git clone https://github.com/OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant.git ~/repos/openclown-assistant
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
```

(Native git proxy auto-rewrites `https://github.com/...` to `https://git-manager.devin.ai/proxy/github.com/...` for you.)

### 1c. Validator green on current main

```bash
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
python3 scripts/validate_docs.py | tail -3
```

Expect: `validated 44 artifact(s); 0 failed`.

If `pyyaml` is missing in the python env (the validator imports it), run `pip install pyyaml` once. The org-level env config (accepted 2026-04-30) already includes `pip install pyyaml` as `maintenance` — fresh sessions in subsequent snapshot builds should not need this.

If validator fails, **stop and tell the PO** — main should never be in a broken validator state.

### 1d. `gh` CLI (Devin's native tools usually replace it)

```bash
gh --version 2>/dev/null && gh auth status 2>/dev/null && echo "GH_OK" || echo "GH_MISSING_OR_UNAUTHED"
```

Use the native tools wherever possible: `git_view_pr` / `git_create_pr` / `git_pr_checks` / `git_ci_job_logs` / `git_comment_on_pr` / `git_update_pr_description`. They are faster and always authenticated.

| Field | Value |

| Repo | `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` (public) |

| Production runtime | OpenClaw skill, TypeScript on Node 24, Docker Compose to a single VPS |
| LLM stack | OmniRoute → Fireworks pool. Architect: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) primary / Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf alt / Opus 4.6 thinking opencode fallback. Executor: GLM 5.1 default; Qwen 3.6 Plus / Codex GPT-5.5 listed but unused in pilot. Reviewer: Kimi K2.6 (single source of truth, never varies). |
| Repo VPS | 6 vCPU / 7.6 GiB RAM / 75 GB / Ubuntu 24.04. Repo path on VPS: `~/repos/openclown-assistant`. |
| Validator | `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py` (`pyyaml` already in env config — `maintenance` section installs it on every snapshot build) |
| Pre-commit hooks | None at this snapshot — verify with `ls .pre-commit-config.yaml .husky/ 2>/dev/null` and act on actual repo state |
| Inline-AI PR review bot | **PR-Agent (`the-pr-agent/pr-agent`) running Qwen 3.6 Plus via OmniRoute / Fireworks**, configured in `.github/workflows/pr_agent.yml` + `.pr_agent.toml`. Auto-runs `auto_review` + `auto_describe` + `auto_improve` on every `opened` / `reopened` / `ready_for_review` PR event. **Replaces** Devin Review (which the PO ran out of ACUs for on 2026-04-30 mid-session). |

## 4. Pipeline (stable)

```
PRD (Business Planner)
  └→ Reviewer #1 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Planner family)
       └→ ArchSpec + ADRs + Tickets (Architect)
            └→ Reviewer #2 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Architect family)
                 └→ Code (Executor; one Ticket per session; GLM/Qwen/Codex)
                      └→ Reviewer #3 (RV-CODE, Kimi K2.6) + PR-Agent inline bot
                           └→ PO merges
```

**Session independence.** Every artifact gets a new LLM session. No reuse across artifacts. **Exception:** Reviewer iter-N must reuse the same Kimi K2.6 opencode-session that produced iter-1 (continuity preserves context for re-eval).

**Branch hygiene for review PRs.** Reviewer sessions branch their review-PR **from `main`**, never from the artifact's own branch (per `docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §A.15 / §B.15). **Known footgun (carried debt, see §6.4 step 4):** when the target artifact is unmerged (e.g. SPEC review of a `proposed` ADR on its own arch-branch), the validator on the rv-branch fails because cross-references (`target_ref`) cannot resolve to a file on the from-`main` rv-branch tree. Workaround until the carried-debt PR lands: rebase the rv-branch onto the artifact's arch-branch BEFORE pushing the iter-N commit. RV-SPEC-005 iter-2 used exactly this workaround on 2026-04-30 (NUDGE-iter-2 instructed Kimi to `git rebase origin/arch/ADR-010-calorie-floor` before re-eval push); validator went from 19 errors → 44/0.

**Q-TKT trigger.** If the spec lacks a decisive answer, Executor MUST create `docs/questions/Q-TKT-<NNN>-<NN>.md` and stop. Do NOT guess. This is codified in `docs/prompts/executor.md`; precedent: Q-TKT-005-01 produced ADR-005@0.2.0 amendment cycle.

**NUDGE iteration model.** When Reviewer / PR-Agent surface findings, Executor (or Reviewer) addresses them in iter-N+1 on the same artifact/PR via continuation message into the existing opencode-session. Verdict scheme (`docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §A.14 / §B.14): `pass` (zero findings), `pass_with_changes` (zero HIGH findings; medium/low may remain deferred per PO), `fail` (≥1 HIGH finding).

---

## 5. Roles and write-zones (canonical: `CONTRIBUTING.md` lines 7-22)

| Role | Default model | Runtime | MAY write | MUST NOT write |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Product Owner (human) | — | — | Anything (final authority) | — |
| Business Planner | GPT-5.5 thinking / Opus 4.7 thinking | ChatGPT Plus / Devin | `docs/prd/` | Everything else |
| Architect | GPT-5.5 xhigh primary / Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf alt | opencode CLI / Windsurf | `docs/architecture/`, `docs/tickets/` | `docs/prd/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `infra/`, repo root |
| Executor | GLM 5.1 default | opencode + OmniRoute | `src/`, `tests/`, append-only `docs/tickets/<id>.md#10 Execution Log`, ticket `status` (4 transitions only), `docs/questions/` | `docs/prd/`, `docs/architecture/`, any other ticket field, anything outside ticket §5 Outputs |
| Reviewer (LLM) | Kimi K2.6 | opencode + OmniRoute | `docs/reviews/` | Everything else; **NEVER `status: approved`** (PO-delegated orchestrator clerical patch sets that based on Reviewer verdict) |
| Reviewer (auto bot) | PR-Agent / Qwen 3.6 Plus via OmniRoute | GitHub Actions | inline PR comments + auto-generated PR descriptions | files in repo |
| **Orchestrator (you)** | Devin | webapp | Coordination + `docs/session-log/` + `docs/backlog/` (light) + ticket frontmatter promotions (`status`, `arch_ref`, `version`, `updated`, plus established closure fields `closed_at`/`closed_by`/`closure_pr`/`closure_commit`/`review_ref` per TKT-001..005 precedent) + RV frontmatter promotion (`status: in_review` → `approved` + `approved_at`/`approved_by`/`approved_after_iters`/`approved_note`) + ADR frontmatter promotion (`status: proposed` → `accepted` + `review_refs`/`reviewer_models`/`approved_at`/`approved_by`/`approved_note` per ADR-005@0.2.0 + ADR-010@0.1.0 precedent) per `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §5 PO-delegated clerical patch carve-out | code, formal artifact bodies (PRD/ARCH/ADR/RV), substantive ticket body edits beyond reference-pinning, `docs/prompts/`, `CONTRIBUTING.md` |

**Orchestrator-write-zone hard rule (CONTRIBUTING.md line 22):** any orchestrator edit to files outside this declared write-zone (e.g. `CONTRIBUTING.md` itself, `docs/prompts/`, `scripts/`, formal artifact bodies) requires **explicit PO authorisation recorded verbatim in the PR body**. Without it Reviewer / PR-Agent will reject the PR.

   - `scripts/validate_docs.py` patch: exempt `target_ref` artifact-id from the existence-check (so SPEC-mode review of a `proposed` ADR on its own arch-branch doesn't fail with 19 errors when the rv-branch is forked from main and ADR-010 isn't there yet). The current behavior caused the iter-1 → iter-2 footgun on RV-SPEC-005.

This formalises the manual workaround used in RV-SPEC-005 iter-2.

   - Skip if PO has not given the green light; this is debt prevention, not a blocker.

- **Hidden findings on PR #41** — Devin Review panel showed «View 2 additional findings» but PO's ACUs were exhausted before they could be enumerated. RV-SPEC-005 `approved_note` documents the deferral. If any prove substantive in retrospective triage, route to a separate clerical follow-up PR.

### 6.6 Tooling assumptions

- Architect: GPT-5.5 xhigh in opencode CLI on PO's VPS (primary; per ratified `docs/prompts/architect.md` post-PR #39 cleanup). Alternatives: Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf / Opus 4.6 thinking opencode.
- Executor: GLM 5.1 in opencode + OmniRoute on PO's VPS. Same opencode-session is reused across iterations of one ticket; new session per ticket.
- Reviewer (LLM): Kimi K2.6 in opencode + OmniRoute on PO's VPS. Same Kimi-session is reused across iterations of one review; new session per artifact.
- Reviewer (auto bot): PR-Agent (Qwen 3.6 Plus via OmniRoute / Fireworks). Auto-runs on every PR. Replaces Devin Review.
- VPS: 6 vCPU / 7.6 GiB RAM / 75 GB / Ubuntu 24.04. Repo path on VPS: `~/repos/openclown-assistant`.
- Devin native git tools (`git_view_pr` / `git_create_pr` / `git_pr_checks` / `git_ci_job_logs`) work via the org's git-manager proxy; `gh` CLI fallback is rarely needed for orchestrator work.

### 6.7 Reviewer bootstrap for RV-CODE-006 (inlined for portability)

The paste-ready file `~/handoff/REVIEWER_BOOTSTRAP_RV-CODE-006.md` may not survive a box restart. The full content is preserved here; copy from this file directly if `~/handoff/` is missing.

> ### How to use
>
> 1. Open a NEW opencode-session on the VPS, model = `kimi-k2.6` (via OmniRoute).
> 2. **First message** in the session = the full contents of `~/repos/openclown-assistant/docs/prompts/reviewer.md` (the role system prompt). Easiest:
>    ```sh
>    cat ~/repos/openclown-assistant/docs/prompts/reviewer.md
>    ```
>    Copy stdout, paste as the first message. Press Enter and let Kimi acknowledge the role.
> 3. **Second message** = everything between the `<<<REVIEWER_MESSAGE_BEGIN>>>` and `<<<REVIEWER_MESSAGE_END>>>` markers below. Markers themselves are NOT pasted.
> 4. Kimi will execute §B CODE WORKFLOW from reviewer.md autonomously.
> 5. When Kimi reports back (PR opened, verdict + top-3 findings summary), pingani the orchestrator with the PR number.
>
> ```
> <<<REVIEWER_MESSAGE_BEGIN>>>
> You are running CODE-mode review per `docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §B. Execute steps §B.1–§B.15 in order, autonomously. Do not stop and ask the PO whether to proceed; the System Prompt you just received IS the approval.
>
> # Target
> - PR: https://github.com/OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant/pull/42
> - Ticket: TKT-006@0.1.0 (docs/tickets/TKT-006-food-lookup-kbju-estimator.md).
> - Executor model: GLM 5.1.
> - Component: C6 KBJU Estimator (per ARCH-001@0.4.0 §3.6, §4.2, §4.8, §6, §9.4).
> - PR title (for §B.2 slug): TKT-006: Food Lookup KBJU Estimator.
> - Review artifact ID (for §B.2 scaffold): RV-CODE-006.
>
> # Inputs you MUST read at the exact versions cited (per Ticket §4)
> - docs/architecture/ARCH-001-kbju-coach-v0-1.md AT VERSION 0.4.0 (current main HEAD). Sections §3.6, §4.2, §4.8, §6, §9.4. Note: TKT-006 frontmatter pins arch_ref: ARCH-001@0.2.0 — the Ticket pin is historical (predates ADR-005 + ADR-010 cascades). The PR was implemented while ARCH-001@0.2.0 was the ratified contract; review whether the implementation respects the contracts that existed at the time of Ticket creation. If @0.4.0 introduces a breaking change vs @0.2.0 for C6 contracts, surface that as a context-finding for orchestrator routing (not Executor defect).
> - docs/architecture/adr/ADR-002-llm-routing-policy.md @0.1.0
> - docs/architecture/adr/ADR-005-hybrid-kbju-estimation.md @0.2.0
> - docs/architecture/adr/ADR-009-...md @0.1.0 (look up filename)
> - docs/knowledge/llm-routing.md (full file)
> - docs/prd/PRD-001-kbju-coach.md (sections referenced from ARCH-001 §3.6 / §6)
> - README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, AGENTS.md (full files)
> - Read-only context: src/shared/types.ts, src/store/tenantStore.ts, src/observability/costGuard.ts, src/observability/events.ts at main HEAD.
>
> # Review scope (apply §B workflow with these emphasises)
> 1. §B.3 Scope compliance. Diff must match TKT-006 §5 Outputs exactly (8 files: src/llm/omniRouteClient.ts, src/kbju/types.ts, src/kbju/foodLookup.ts, src/kbju/kbjuEstimator.ts, src/kbju/validation.ts, tests/kbju/foodLookup.test.ts, tests/kbju/kbjuEstimator.test.ts, tests/kbju/validation.test.ts). Ticket file permitted iff edits limited to status frontmatter + §10 Execution Log append per HARD SCOPE carve-out. **Use the two-dot diff `git diff origin/main..origin/tkt/TKT-006-food-lookup-kbju-estimator` for ground truth — the symmetric three-dot diff will mislead because the Executor's branch ancestry is non-canonical (forked from rv/RV-SPEC-005-... not main).**
> 2. §B.4 Dependency compliance. TKT-006 §7 forbids new runtime deps. Verify package.json unchanged.
> 3. §B.5 AC verification. All 8 ACs (TKT-006 §6). Run npm test, npm run lint, npm run typecheck, python3 scripts/validate_docs.py yourself. Note: npm run lint and npm run typecheck both alias to `tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json` (intentional repo configuration; not Executor defect — surface as context-finding for orchestrator if you think AC for "lint" should run an actual ESLint pass).
> 4. §B.6 Contract compliance. ARCH-001@0.4.0 §4.8 specifies the C6 KBJU estimator path (Open Food Facts → USDA → LLM via OmniRoute fallback). §9.4 specifies the LLM call envelope. §6 specifies what the estimator returns / persists.
> 5. §B.7 Code quality. Readability, error handling on every external call, no TODOs without §"Follow-up TKT suggestions" entry, no commented-out code, no debug logging.
> 6. §B.10 Security. Russian-language input fed to OFF / USDA / LLM — adversarial prompt-injection surface? KBJU estimator must serialize user text as data fields with fixed system/developer instructions per ARCH-001@0.4.0 §9.4 + TKT-006 §7. Verify. Are prompts and provider responses kept out of C10 logs (TKT-006 AC #8)? Verify by reading actual log/trace emission paths.
> 7. §B.13 Hostile-reader pass. Concurrent food-lookup requests for same item: race on cache write? OmniRoute 429 retry behaviour: does retryOnce actually back off? Suspicious-output detection: can adversarial input bypass the "no retry on suspicious output" guard?
>
> # Workflow notes
> - Branch base. Per §B.15: git fetch origin && git checkout -b rv/RV-CODE-006-tkt-006-food-lookup-kbju-estimator origin/main from a CLEAN tree. Do NOT branch from tkt/TKT-006-...
> - Iteration model. Default: emit verdict on iter-1 with all findings catalogued. If iter-1 is `pass` or `pass_with_changes`, you are done. If `fail`, write iter-1 verdict and stop.
> - HTTPS push fallback. If git push -u origin rv/RV-CODE-006-... fails 403: switch remote to SSH per docs/prompts/reviewer.md GIT PUSH FALLBACK, push, switch back.
>
> # Stop conditions
> 1. Review file exists at docs/reviews/RV-CODE-006-tkt-006-food-lookup-kbju-estimator.md.
> 2. Frontmatter has status: in_review, id: RV-CODE-006, type: code_review, target_pr, ticket_ref: TKT-006@0.1.0, reviewer_model: "kimi-k2.6", created.
> 3. All findings catalogued by severity (high/medium/low) with file:line references and concrete fix recommendations.
> 4. Top-level verdict: pass / pass_with_changes / fail.
> 5. python3 scripts/validate_docs.py green on your branch (45/0 expected if PR #43 merged; 44/0 otherwise — both acceptable).
> 6. Branch pushed; PR opened with title "RV-CODE-006: Review TKT-006 Food Lookup KBJU Estimator (PR #42)" + body containing verdict + top-3 findings + PO recommendation.
> 7. Review file status: in_review (orchestrator flips to approved in separate clerical PR).
>
> Begin.
> <<<REVIEWER_MESSAGE_END>>>
> ```

---

## 7. After self-checks pass + required reading done

Reply to the PO with:

1. **5-line state summary** in your own words (proves you read this file + the current ArchSpec + open Tickets + PR #42 state)
2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the orchestrator. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs/ArchSpecs/ADRs, and will only modify `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), and ticket / RV / ADR frontmatter promotion fields. Other CONTRIBUTING.md edits require explicit PO authorisation, recorded in the PR body."
3. **One concrete proposed next action** with reasoning ("do X *because* Y trade-off"), citing the artifact / ADR / ticket that motivates it. The default obvious next action is §6.4 step 1 (RV-CODE-006 dispatch).
4. **Wait for the PO to say "go"** before executing.

---

## 8. If something contradicts

If this file disagrees with `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` or with `CONTRIBUTING.md` — **the repo files win, this snapshot loses**. Snapshots are volatile by definition; the repo is the source of truth across handoffs. Tell the PO what you found.
